name: Unnamed Narrator
role: A lawyer and former Master in Chancery who employs Bartleby.

name: Bartleby
role: A pale, sedentary scrivener who eventually refuses to perform any work.

name: John Jacob Astor
role: A wealthy man and former client of the narrator.

name: Turkey
role: An elderly English copyist who becomes rash and erratic in the afternoons.

name: Nippers
role: A young, ambitious copyist suffering from indigestion and irritability.

name: Ginger Nut
role: A twelve-year-old office boy and errand runner.

name: Ginger Nut's Father
role: A carman who sent his son to the office as a student at law.

name: The Dun
role: A man posing as Nippers' client who is actually a debt collector.

name: The Cleaning Woman
role: A woman residing in the attic who cleans the narrator's chambers.

name: The Lawyer Tenant
role: A tenant at No.—Wall-street who is distressed by Bartleby's presence.

name: The Landlord
role: The portly owner of the building at No.—Wall-street.

name: Mr. Cutlets
role: A prison food provider at the Tombs.

name: Monroe Edwards
role: A known forger mentioned by the grub-man.

name: The turnkeys
role: Prison officers at the Tombs who interact with the narrator and Bartleby.
